items.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime U.S. Federal Reserve chairman, dies aged 100
items.summary: Greenspan, who exerted a powerful influence on the U.S. economy during his tenure at the helm of the ​Fed from August 1987 to January 2006, died at his home from complications of Parkinson’s Disease

items.title: What is downblending, the process at the heart of Trump’s Iran agreement?
items.summary: The international community’s trust in Iran’s promise to not pursue a nuclear weapon depends on how well it implements downblending in the coming months

items.title: U.S. and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say
items.summary: A joint statement from mediating nations ​Qatar and Pakistan said the U.S. and Iran agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Mediators hail 'progress' in U.S.-Iran talks after lengthy opening session
items.summary: The host nation said conditions were set for technical talks to immediately follow, with the sides at the beginning of 60 days to secure a long-term settlement

items.title: Keir Starmer sixth U.K. PM to resign after Brexit
items.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation, following months of internal conflict within the Conservative Party.

items.title: Keir Starmer announces his resignation as U.K. Prime Minister
items.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer had informed King Charles III of his decision on Monday morning, following a weekend of deliberation at his official country home, Chequers

items.title: Russian drone strike in Ukraine kills three from one family, including 13-year-old boy
items.summary: Russia has pounded civilian areas of Ukraine with drones and missiles since it launched its all-out invasion of its neighbour more than four years ago, and a United Nations tally says more than 16,000 civilians have died in the war

items.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo outbreak top 1,000 with 254 deaths, authorities say
items.summary: Contact tracing remains a key issue for local authorities, who have only achieved a 55% coverage rate, the Congo’s Ministry of Health said

items.title: 2 senior U.S. officials visit Sri Lanka amid growing focus on Indian Ocean region

items.summary: Speaking at a joint press conference with the Bangladeshi Prime Minister, Anwar Ibrahim described "human resources cooperation, particularly the workers" as "critical for our survival"

items.summary: In total, Russian defence systems downed ​301 drones overnight, local newswires said, ‌citing the Defence Ministry. That tally included Russia-occupied areas

items.title: Europe sweats through new heatwave, with worse to come
items.summary: Scientists have shown that recurring heatwaves are a clear marker of global warming, primarily caused by burning coal, oil and gas — and warn they are set to become more frequent, longer and more intense

items.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 2, leaves 6 survivors, in eastern Pacific Ocean
items.summary: The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to more than 210 people since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls “narcoterrorists” in early September

items.title: Who is Abelardo De La Espriella, Colombia's new right-wing President?
items.summary: Nicknamed “The Tiger” by his followers, Mr. De La Espriella portrayed himself as an anti-establishment savior capable of reviving Colombia’s ailing economy and restoring order in a country rattled by illegal armed groups and drug trafficking

items.title: Thousands of Czechs rally against government plan to overhaul funding of public broadcasters
items.summary: According to the plan approved by the government, public radio and television would be financed from the state budget starting next year, and not from fees paid by individuals, households and businesses

items.title: Ramiro Valdes, lauded as hero of Cuban revolution, dies at 94
items.summary: Exiled with Castro in Mexico, he was ⁠one of 82 men who sailed the yacht Granma to Cuba in 1956 to restart the insurrection - and one of only 12 to survive

items.title: Ukraine's battlefield shift has not solved its humanitarian crisis, IRC says
items.summary: David Miliband, the IRC’s President and CEO, said ⁠it was still vital to recognise the impact of aid cuts, led by the U.S., that have halved the IRC’s budget in the country to a current ​estimate of $20 million in 2027 from $40 ‌million last year.

items.title: Wife of detained ex-Sri Lankan intelligence chief alleges harassment by agencies
items.summary: I've been informed by my children that upon leaving the hospital premises, a CID officer has entered the lift with them and appeared to follow them, says Manori Sallay, ex- Sri Lanka's State Intelligence Service head Suresh Sallay's wife

items.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
items.summary: Governor Sergey Aksyonov, the Kremlin-appointed head of Crimea, said that overnight Ukrainian strikes killed four people and wounded 28 others. He did not specify the target of the attack.

items.title: Colombians vote in Presidential runoff
items.summary: Up to 41 million voters will choose between frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella and his leftist rival Ivan Cepeda — the latest in a series of hyper-polarized Latin American elections.

items.title: Bolivia crisis begins to ease after lawmakers back state of emergency
